data.title: OpenAI's Revenue Projections for 2030 Surpass Previous Estimates
data.description: According to PANews, OpenAI's revenue projections for 2030 are expected to exceed previous forecasts by approximately 15%. The company anticipates consuming over $8 billion this year, which is $1.5 billion more than earlier predictions. OpenAI is projected to generate nearly $10 billion in revenue through ChatGPT this year, with total annual revenue expected to reach $13 billion.

data.title: SEC Delays Decision on Grayscale Polkadot ETF Application
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has postponed its decision regarding the application for a Grayscale Polkadot (DOT) spot ETF. The delay comes as the SEC continues to evaluate the proposal, which aims to offer investors exposure to Polkadot through a regulated exchange-traded fund. This decision is part of the SEC's ongoing scrutiny of cryptocurrency-related financial products, reflecting its cautious approach to integrating digital assets into traditional investment frameworks.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Sees Notable Gains and Losses
data.description:
According to PANews, several cryptocurrencies have experienced significant price movements. Leading the gains, MKR is currently priced at $1,796.8, marking a 5.09% increase. W follows with a price of $0.0794, up by 4.02%. PEOPLE is trading at $0.0191, reflecting a 3.97% rise, while ILV is at $14.093, up by 3.95%. WLD has also seen a 3.08% increase, now priced at $0.913.

Conversely, some cryptocurrencies have faced declines. MANA is currently priced at $0.317, experiencing a 0.59% decrease. AR is trading at $6.566, down by 0.44%. TRX has seen a 0.39% drop, now at $0.331. APT is priced at $4.244, reflecting a 0.31% decrease, and RON is at $0.471, down by 0.23%.

data.title: SOL Strategies was approved to list on the Nasdaq on September 9 under the ticker symbol STKE.
data.description: SOL Strategies (HODL), a Toronto-listed digital asset company focused on the Solana blockchain, has received approval to list its common shares on the Nasdaq Global Select Market. Trading of the shares will begin on September 9th under the ticker symbol STKE. As of the end of August, the company had 435,064 SOL tokens. The shares, previously listed on the Canadian Securities Exchange (CSE), will continue to trade under the ticker symbol HODL but will be delisted from the U.S. over-the-counter (OTC) market, where they previously traded under the ticker symbol CYFRF. OTC stock holders do not need to take any action as their shares will automatically convert to Nasdaq-listed shares. (Coindesk)

data.title: US Senate updates crypto bill to block tokenized stocks from getting a ‘commodity’ label
data.description:
The Senate just stuffed a brand new clause into its crypto bill to stop tokenized stocks from being labeled as commodities, closing in on a regulatory wall that could box crypto firms in. The update came Friday, with a new provision inside the draft of the Responsible Financial Innovation Act of 2025. The goal? Draw […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/senate-block-tokenized-stocks-commodity/

data.title: U.S. Regulators Propose Supportive Measures for Cryptocurrency Markets
data.description:
According to PANews, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chairman Paul Atkins and Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) Acting Chairman Caroline Pham have jointly proposed several initiatives aimed at supporting cryptocurrency markets. Among these is a proposal for a '24/7 market' similar to traditional financial markets, designed to align the U.S. economy with the pace of digital asset markets.

This policy would enable continuous online trading on stock exchanges. For 154 years since Wall Street introduced continuous trading, such markets have adhered to strict trading schedules, open only during specific business hours on weekdays since 1985. However, the chairpersons indicated that adjustments might be necessary to keep up with constantly active markets like cryptocurrency, gold, and foreign exchange.

The chairpersons also suggested easing restrictions for 'innovators' to launch event contracts in prediction markets and allowing perpetual derivatives contracts—common in offshore cryptocurrency markets but currently heavily restricted in the U.S.—to be freely traded between securities and commodity exchanges. Another proposal involves establishing an 'innovation exemption' for DeFi protocols offering spot cryptocurrency and perpetual derivatives contract trading. The chairpersons noted that these proposals align with a report released by the Trump administration in July, which directed agencies to relax many restrictions on U.S. cryptocurrency trading.

data.title: WLFI Addresses Concerns Over Recent Wallet Blacklisting
data.description:
According to PANews, WLFI has responded to community concerns regarding the recent blacklisting of wallets, emphasizing that normal activities will not be suppressed. In recent days, 272 wallets have been blacklisted, representing a small fraction of total holders. This action aims to prevent user losses, with investigations underway to assist affected users.

The breakdown of the 272 wallets is as follows: 215 cases, approximately 79.0%, are related to phishing attacks. The team has intervened to prevent hackers from stealing funds and is collaborating with legitimate owners to protect or transfer assets. Fifty cases, about 18.4%, involve owners reporting violations, prompting the team to blacklist these addresses at their request to help protect or recover funds. Five cases, roughly 1.8%, are marked as high-risk exposure, with security risks under review. One case, around 0.4%, involves alleged misappropriation of other holders' funds, with a comprehensive internal review in progress.

WLFI assures that normal trading activities will not be banned and will act immediately upon receiving alerts of malicious or high-risk activities that may harm community members. Future measures include continued collaboration with legitimate owners to verify control and ensure fund security. Upon review completion, clear results for each category will be published. Any broader actions affecting holders will be publicly announced.

data.title: Ethena Expands StablecoinX PIPE Transaction with $530 Million Investment
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Ethena has announced an additional $530 million investment in the "StablecoinX" PIPE transaction. The foundation is also initiating a new $310 million open market repurchase plan.

Previously, BlockBeats reported that on July 21, stablecoin issuer StablecoinX completed a $360 million financing round aimed at acquiring ENA tokens. The company plans to list its Class A common stock on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker symbol "USDE." Ethena Foundation will contribute ENA tokens valued at $60 million.

To facilitate the acquisition plan, StablecoinX will utilize the net cash proceeds of $260 million from the financing, after deducting related expenses, to purchase locked ENA tokens from a subsidiary of the Ethena Foundation.

data.title: Tokenized asset management platform Plural completes $7.13 million seed round, led by Paradigm
data.description: Tokenized asset management platform Plural announced the completion of a $7.13 million seed round led by Paradigm, with participation from Maven 11, Volt Capital, and Neoclassic Capital, bringing its total funding to nearly $10 million. Plural is building financial infrastructure for the digital economy. Through tokenization and smart contract automation, Plural transforms real-world energy assets like solar, batteries, and data centers into scalable, programmable investment products. (PR Newswire)

data.title: SOL Strategies to Begin Trading on Nasdaq Global Select Market
data.description:
According to PANews, SOL Strategies, a digital asset company focused on the Solana blockchain and listed in Toronto, has received approval to list its common stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market. Trading is set to commence on September 9 under the ticker symbol STKE. As of the end of August, the company held 435,064 SOL tokens.

Previously listed on the Canadian Securities Exchange (CSE) under the ticker HODL, the company's shares will continue trading on the CSE but will be delisted from the U.S. over-the-counter (OTC) market, where they were traded under the symbol CYFRF. OTC shareholders do not need to take any action, as their shares will automatically convert to Nasdaq-listed stock.
